output.raw_output:
{
  "email_sequence": [
    {
      "email_number": 1,
      "subject_line": "Your landlord is out of time—recover your security deposit now",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],

Still waiting on your security deposit? State law gives landlords a hard deadline to return your money. If they miss it, you could be entitled to your full deposit plus penalties.

The fastest way to force a response is a formal security deposit demand letter. Unlike a text or casual email, a demand letter creates a legal paper trail that landlords can't ignore.

DepositBack builds your letter in minutes, citing the exact statutes for your state so you don't have to guess.

[Generate your demand letter now]

Best,
The DepositBack Team"
    },
    {
      "email_number": 2,
      "subject_line": "Free security deposit demand letter template inside",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],

You shouldn't need a lawyer to get your money back.

That's why we built a free security deposit demand letter template that handles the hard part for you. Just enter your state, move-out date, and a few rental details.

In under 3 minutes, you'll have a professional, state-specific security deposit letter to landlord ready to send via certified mail or email.

Whether you need a security deposit refund letter or a full demand package, our tool formats everything correctly so your landlord takes it seriously.

[Get your free template]

Best,
The DepositBack Team"
    },
    {
      "email_number": 3,
      "subject_line": "Landlord won't return your security deposit? Do this immediately",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],

If your landlord won't return your security deposit, waiting only weakens your position. Evidence disappears, deadlines pass, and landlords know most tenants give up.

A demand letter for security deposit recovery changes the game. It proves you know your rights and creates the paper trail small claims courts want to see before you escalate.

Our users recover an average of $1,400 in withheld deposits. The common thread? They sent a formal letter, not a casual request.

[Send your letter today]

Best,
The DepositBack Team"
    },
    {
      "email_number": 4,
      "subject_line": "Your security deposit return letter is ready to send",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],

Time is your biggest enemy in deposit recovery.

Every day you delay, your landlord assumes you've moved on. A security deposit return letter—or security deposit refund letter—puts the legal obligation in writing and forces them to respond with valid deductions or return your money.

DepositBack auto-fills state deadlines, required legal language, and delivery instructions. No research, no formatting, no stress.

The longer you wait, the harder it gets to recover what's yours.

[Create your return letter now]

Best,
The DepositBack Team"
    },
    {
      "email_number": 5,
      "subject_line": "Last chance: Write your letter to landlord about security deposit recovery",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],

This is your final reminder: writing a letter to landlord about security deposit recovery doesn't have to be complicated.

Stop researching how to write a security deposit letter from scratch. Stop second-guessing your wording. DepositBack asks 5 simple questions and generates a legally sound, state-specific demand letter tailored to your situation.

You've already done the hard part—moving out, cleaning, and handing over the keys. Now let us handle the paperwork so you get paid.

[Start your letter now]

Best,
The DepositBack Team"
    }
  ],
  "title": "Security Deposit Demand Letter | Free State-Specific Template | DepositBack",
  "meta_description": "Create a free security deposit demand letter in minutes. State-specific templates to help renters recover deposits fast—no lawyer needed.",
  "content_outline": [
    "Introduction: The security deposit problem and why landlords ignore informal requests",
    "What is a security deposit demand letter and why it works",

    "Free security deposit demand letter template and sample copy",
    "What to do when your landlord won't return your security deposit",
    "Security deposit return letter vs. security deposit refund letter: key differences",
    "How to send your security deposit letter to landlord for maximum impact",
    "DepositBack tool walkthrough: generate your letter in under 5 minutes"
  ],
  "key_points": [
    "Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–60 days after move-out",
    "A formal demand letter creates a legal paper trail and is often required before small claims court",
    "DepositBack generates state-specific security deposit demand letters instantly using proven legal formatting",
    "No lawyer is needed to draft an effective letter, but state statute citations dramatically increase recovery rates"
  ],
